title: Kakita Bushi Comprehensive Study
question: What is the optimal overall strategy for a Kakita Bushi, and does it change by opponent?
description: Simulates thousands of duels to answer: how should a Kakita Bushi spend Void Points, when should they attack, and does the answer change depending on the opponent? Tests every combination of VP spending strategy, action timing, interrupt attacks, and character build across 4 opponents (Akodo, Bayushi, Shiba, Wave Man) at 7 experience levels. Each 'win rate' is the percentage of simulated duels won by the Kakita.

variables.name: build
variables.label: Build Order

options.name: baseline
options.label: Baseline
options.description: Default Kakita priority order: raises Dan-relevant skills first, then rings in order of combat utility.

options.name: swap_earth_water
options.label: Swap Earth/Water
options.description: Swaps the priority of Earth and Water ring purchases. Water is raised early and Earth late, instead of the default Earth-first order.

options.name: rush_dan4
options.label: Rush Dan 4
options.description: Purchases Dan 4 skills (Double Attack, Iaijutsu 4, Lunge, Attack 4, Parry 4) before raising any rank-3 rings.

options.name: delay_dan4
options.label: Delay Dan 4
options.description: Raises all rank-3 rings (Void, Air, Water) before purchasing any Dan 4 skills.

variables.description: The order in which XP is spent on rings, skills, and Dan advancement

variables.name: attack_style
variables.label: Attack Style

options.name: std_novp
options.label: Standard, No Attack VP
options.description: Standard Kakita attacks (no interrupt), never spending VP on attack rolls.

options.name: std_vp05
options.label: Standard, Attack VP 0.5
options.description: Standard Kakita attacks (no interrupt), spending VP on attack rolls with a 0.5 confidence threshold.

options.name: std_vp07
options.label: Standard, Attack VP 0.7
options.description: Standard Kakita attacks (no interrupt), spending VP on attack rolls with the default 0.7 confidence threshold.

options.name: int_novp
options.label: Interrupt, No Attack VP
options.description: Kakita interrupt attacks enabled, never spending VP on attack rolls.

options.name: int_vp05
options.label: Interrupt, Attack VP 0.5
options.description: Kakita interrupt attacks enabled, spending VP on attack rolls with a 0.5 confidence threshold.

options.name: int_vp07
options.label: Interrupt, Attack VP 0.7
options.description: Kakita interrupt attacks enabled, spending VP on attack rolls with the default 0.7 confidence threshold.

variables.description: Combined interrupt mode and attack VP spending. Flattened from 2 interrupt options x 3 attack VP options to avoid conflicting 'attack' strategy overrides.

variables.name: action_hold
variables.label: Action Timing

options.name: hold
options.label: Hold One Action
options.description: Holds one action die in reserve until Phase 10 or until multiple actions are available, preserving flexibility to react.

options.name: immediate
options.label: Always Attack
options.description: Attacks immediately whenever any action is available, maximizing offensive pressure without holding back.

variables.description: Whether to hold one action in reserve or attack immediately whenever an action is available.

variables.name: wound_check_vp
variables.label: Wound Check VP

options.name: never
options.label: Never Spend
options.description: Never spends Void Points on wound checks, saving them entirely for attack rolls.

options.name: threshold_05
options.label: Threshold 0.5
options.description: Spends VP on wound checks with a 0.5 confidence threshold — spends when moderately likely to help.

variables.description: Whether to spend Void Points on wound checks and at what confidence threshold.

variables.name: interrupt
variables.label: Interrupt Attack
variables.description: Whether to use the Kakita interrupt attack technique (extracted from the flattened attack_style dimension).

variables.name: attack_vp
variables.label: Attack VP Spending

options.name: never
options.label: Never
options.description: Never spend VP on attack rolls.

options.name: threshold_05
options.label: Threshold 0.5
options.description: Spend VP on attacks with 0.5 confidence threshold.

options.name: threshold_07
options.label: Threshold 0.7
options.description: Spend VP on attacks with 0.7 confidence threshold (default).

variables.description: How aggressively Void Points are spent on attack rolls (extracted from the flattened attack_style dimension).
